2017	\begin{proof}
2018	  \uses{lem:free_cech_engine}
2019	  The complex \(C_\bullet\) is the chain/coproduct dual of the constant-coefficient
2020	  core of Lemma~\ref{lem:free_cech_engine}. The differential
2021	  \(\operatorname{cechEngineD}\) is defined on coproduct injections by the
2022	  alternating-sign index-drop \(\sigma \mapsto \sigma \circ \operatorname{Fin.succAbove}
2023	  i\); both \(d \circ d = 0\) and the contracting identity
2024	  \(s \circ d + d \circ s = \mathrm{id}\) are proved by extending along the
2025	  injections with \(\operatorname{Limits.Sigma.hom\_ext}\) and reducing each
2026	  injection-component to the corresponding combinatorial cancellation of
2027	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combDifferential\_comp}\) respectively
2028	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy\_spec}\) (the prepend
2029	  \(\operatorname{cechEnginePrepend}\) is the coproduct dual of
2030	  \(\operatorname{combHomotopy}\)). Positive-degree exactness
2031	  \(\operatorname{cechEngineD\_exact}\) reads off the contracting identity. The four
2032	  object-half bricks are immediate: evaluation preserves \(\coprod\) and the surviving
2033	  summands are picked out by \(\operatorname{survivingEquiv}\) under the criterion
2034	  \(\operatorname{le\_coverInterOpen\_iff}\), the rest being zero objects
2035	  (\(\operatorname{cechFreeEvalDropZeros}\)).
2036	\end{proof}

2067	  % and

2096	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:free_cech_engine}). The isomorphism is \emph{degreewise} and

2109	\begin{proof}
2110	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise, lem:free_cech_engine}
2111	  \emph{Degreewise.} By \(\operatorname{cechFreeEval\_X}\) of
2112	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise} evaluation at \(V\) commutes with the
2113	  coproduct,
2114	  \((\operatorname{eval} V)(K(\mathcal{U})_p) \cong \coprod_{\sigma :
2115	  \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \mathcal{U}.I_0} (\operatorname{eval} V)(\operatorname{free}
2116	  (\mathbf{y}\,U_\sigma))\). Split the index set into the \(\sigma\) landing in
2117	  \(I_1(V)\) and the rest. Each summand with \(\sigma\) valued in \(I_1(V)\) (so
2118	  \(V \leq U_\sigma\)) is \(\cong \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) by
2119	  \(\operatorname{freeYonedaEval\_iso\_of\_le}\); each remaining summand is the zero
2120	  object by \(\operatorname{freeYonedaEval\_isZero\_of\_not\_le}\). Hence the coproduct
2121	  collapses to \(\coprod_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1(V)} \mathcal{O}_X(V) =
2122	  C_p\).
2123	
2124	  \emph{Differential match.} The evaluated differential is \((\operatorname{eval} V)\)
2125	  applied to the alternating face sum
2126	  \(\operatorname{AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD}(\operatorname{cechFreeSimplicial}
2127	  \mathcal{U})\). The face \(\delta_i\) reindexes the \(\sigma\)-summand by
2128	  \(\sigma \mapsto \sigma \circ \operatorname{Fin.succAbove} i\) (the face action of
2129	  \(\operatorname{cechFreeSimplicial}\)); restricted to the
2130	  \(I_1\)-summands this is exactly the index-drop that the engine encodes, and the sign
2131	  \((-1)^i\) is supplied by \(\operatorname{objD}\). The match is verified on the
2132	  coproduct injections by \(\operatorname{Limits.Sigma.hom\_ext}\), reusing the
2133	  naturality of \(\operatorname{Limits.PreservesCoproduct.iso}\); summing with signs
2134	  identifies the evaluated differential with
2135	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combDifferential}\). This is the hard content of the
2136	  identification and the chain/coproduct dual of the cochain coface match in
2137	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}.
2138	
2139	  \emph{The three layers commuted past.} The degreewise iso is the \(3\)-fold
2140	  composite
2141	  \[
2142	    (\operatorname{eval} V)(K(\mathcal{U})_p)
2143	      \xrightarrow{\;\operatorname{cechFreeEval\_X}\;}
2144	      \coprod_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \mathcal{U}.I_0}
2145	        (\operatorname{eval} V)(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_\sigma))
2146	      \xrightarrow{\;\operatorname{cechFreeEvalDropZeros}\;}
2147	      \coprod_{V \leq U_\sigma} \mathcal{O}_X(V)
2148	      \xrightarrow{\;(\operatorname{Limits.Sigma.whiskerEquiv}\ \ldots)^{-1}\;}
2149	      C_p,
2150	  \]
2151	  whose layers are: (1) \(\operatorname{cechFreeEval\_X}\) = the comparison
2152	  \(\operatorname{Limits.PreservesCoproduct.iso}\) expressing that evaluation at
2153	  \(V\) commutes with the coproduct \(\coprod\); (2) \(\operatorname{cechFreeEvalDropZeros}\),
2154	  which kills the summands with \(V \not\leq U_\sigma\) (these evaluate to zero objects
2155	  by \(\operatorname{freeYonedaEval\_isZero\_of\_not\_le}\)); (3)
2156	  \((\operatorname{Limits.Sigma.whiskerEquiv}\ \operatorname{survivingEquiv}\
2157	  (\operatorname{freeYonedaEval\_iso\_of\_le}\, \ldots))^{-1}\), which reindexes the
2158	  surviving summands by \(\operatorname{survivingEquiv}\) of
2159	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_engine_complex} and identifies each evaluated summand with the
2160	  single coefficient \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) via \(\operatorname{freeYonedaEval\_iso\_of\_le}\).
2161	
2162	  \emph{Naturality past each layer.} The reindex \(\operatorname{survivingEquiv}\) is
2163	  natural in the face map \(\sigma \mapsto \sigma \circ \operatorname{Fin.succAbove} i\):
2164	  a surviving index \(\sigma\) (i.e.\ \(V \leq U_\sigma\), equivalently
2165	  \(V \leq \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\, \mathcal{U}\, \sigma\) by
2166	  \(\operatorname{le\_coverInterOpen\_iff}\)) is carried to a surviving
2167	  \(\sigma \circ \operatorname{Fin.succAbove} i\) (still \(V \leq U_{\sigma \circ
2168	  \operatorname{succAbove}\, i}\), since dropping a coordinate only weakens the meet),
2169	  so the drop-zeros layer (2) and the whisker layer (3) commute with every face. On a
2170	  surviving summand the evaluated transition \(\operatorname{freeYoneda}.\mathrm{map}
2171	  (\operatorname{homOfLE}\, \ldots)\) collapses to the identity under
2172	  \(\operatorname{freeYonedaEval\_iso\_of\_le}\); hence each face \(\delta_i\) matches the
2173	  engine reindexing \(\sigma \mapsto \sigma \circ \operatorname{Fin.succAbove} i\)
2174	  summand-for-summand, and summing with the \(\operatorname{objD}\) signs \((-1)^i\)
2175	  identifies the evaluated alternating-face differential with
2176	  \(\operatorname{cechEngineD}\) (\(= \) the chain transpose of
2177	  \(\operatorname{combDifferential}\)).
2178	
2179	  \emph{The genuine difficulty: variance.} The free \emph{chain} differential
2180	  \emph{lowers} \(\mathrm{Fin}\)-arity (\(\sigma \mapsto \sigma \circ
2181	  \operatorname{Fin.succAbove} i\), faces), whereas
2182	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combDifferential}\) \emph{raises} it (it inserts an
2183	  index, summing over \(i \in I_1\)). The two are reconciled because on the finite
2184	  biproduct \(\coprod = \prod\) the insertion/deletion adjunction makes the index-drop
2185	  chain differential the transpose of the index-raise cochain differential; this
2186	  transpose is precisely what \(\operatorname{cechEngineD}\) of
2187	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_engine_complex} packages, and verifying the commutation amounts to
2188	  the injectionwise check \(\operatorname{Limits.Sigma.hom\_ext}\) outlined above.
2189	\end{proof}

2318	  % lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso.
2319	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy, lem:free_cech_engine, lem:cech_engine_complex, lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso}
2320	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
2321	  % (proof, the $dh + hd = \mathrm{id}$ computation) (read from
2322	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1272--1285).
2323	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Then we compute
2324	  % $(dh + hd)(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_0 \ldots i_p} +
2325	  % \sum_{j \geq 1}\sum_{i \in I_1}
2326	  % (-1)^j (i_0 = i_{\text{fix}}) s_{i_1 \ldots i_{j - 1} i i_j \ldots i_p}
2327	  % + (i_0 = i_{\text{fix}}) d(s)_{i_1 \ldots i_p}$
2328	  % which is equal to $s_{i_0 \ldots i_p}$ as desired."
2329	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
2330	  (the contracting identity).}
2331	  In the situation of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy}
2332	  (\(I_1(V) \neq \varnothing\), \(i_{\mathrm{fix}} \in I_1\) fixed), the transported
2333	  prepend homotopy \(h\) satisfies the contracting identity
2334	  \[
2335	    d \circ h + h \circ d = \mathrm{id}
2336	  \]
2337	  on the augmented evaluated complex
2338	  \(\cdots \to K(\mathcal{U})_1(V) \to K(\mathcal{U})_0(V) \to
2339	  \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) \to 0\) (including the degree-\(0\)/degree-\((-1)\)
2340	  augmentation term). Here \(d\) is the alternating-sum {\v C}ech differential of
2341	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}.
2342	\end{lemma}
2343	
2344	\begin{proof}
2345	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy, lem:free_cech_engine}
2346	  This is the same alternating-sum cancellation as
2347	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy\_spec}\)
2348	  (\(\operatorname{combDifferential}(\operatorname{combHomotopy} r\, t) +
2349	  \operatorname{combHomotopy} r\, (\operatorname{combDifferential} t) = t\)),
2350	  specialised to coefficients \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\). Expanding
2351	  \((dh + hd)(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\): the \(j = 0\) term of \(dh\) prepends and then
2352	  removes \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\), returning \(s_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\); every remaining term
2353	  of \(dh\) cancels in pairs against the corresponding term of \(hd\) (the
2354	  prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) of the differential), because removing an index
2355	  \(j \geq 1\) after prepending \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) equals prepending
2356	  \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) after removing index \(j-1\), with opposite signs. The net
2357	  result is \(s_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\), i.e.\ \(dh + hd = \mathrm{id}\); the index
2358	  bookkeeping is the \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.cons\_comp\_succAbove\_succ}\) /
2359	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combSign\_flip}\) content of
2360	  Lemma~\ref{lem:free_cech_engine}.
2361	\end{proof}

2363	\begin{lemma}
2364	
2365	[Evaluated free complex over an open meeting a cover member]
2366	  \label{lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty}
2367	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_nonempty,
2368	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechEngineComplexAug_quasiIso,

2379	    lem:cech_engine_complex}
2380	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}

2382	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1224--1245).
2383	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "In fact, we claim that for every $W \subset X$ the complex
2384	  % $K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$ is homotopy equivalent to the zero complex."
2385	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
2386	  (homotopy equivalence to zero).}
2387	  With the notation of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}, suppose
2388	  \(I_1(V) \neq \varnothing\). Then the evaluation at \(V\) of
2389	  \(\operatorname{cechFreeComplexAug}\) is a quasi-isomorphism: the augmented
2390	  evaluated complex is contractible, hence its homology is concentrated in degree
2391	  \(0\) where it equals \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\).
2392	
2393	  The parallel \texttt{...Fam} declarations pinned above are the cover-agnostic
2394	  raw-finite-family mirror of this nonempty-index case (an arbitrary finite family of
2395	  opens, no covering hypothesis), feeding the family form of the {\v C}ech
2396	  acyclicity lemma.
2397	\end{lemma}

9180	
9181	\begin{lemma}
9182	[Affine open immersions have vanishing higher direct images]
9183	  \label{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_acyclic}
9184	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic,
9185	    AlgebraicGeometry.isAffineHom_of_affine_separated}
9186	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf,
9187	    lem:sheafify_kills_locally_zero,
9188	    lem:isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero, lem:ext_homcomplex_mathlib,
9189	    lem:isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_sections_locally_zero, lem:pushforward_sections_functor,
9190	    lem:rightDerivedNatIso, lem:sectionsFunctorCorepIso,
9191	    lem:affine_serre_vanishing_general_open, lem:modules_isoSpec_ext_transport,
9192	    lem:isoSpec_scheme_mathlib,
9193	    lem:isZero_coyoneda_rightDerived_of_forall_ext_eq_zero,
9194	    lem:ext_jShriekOU_eq_zero_of_specIso}
9195	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
9196	  % \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative vanishing of higher direct
9197	  % images for affine morphisms) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
9198	  % L180--199).
9199	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
9200	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
9201	  % If $f$ is affine then $R^if_*\mathcal{F} = 0$ for all $i > 0$."
9202	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
9203	  \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing}.}
9204	  Let \(X\) be separated and \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\) the open immersion of an affine
9205	  open \(U\). Then \(j\) is an affine morphism (an open immersion of an affine open into a
9206	  separated scheme), and its higher direct images vanish:
9207	  \[
9208	    R^q j_*\mathcal{H} = 0 \qquad \text{for all } q \geq 1
9209	  \]
9210	  and every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module \(\mathcal{H}\).
9211	\end{lemma}
9212	
9213	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "According to
9214	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
9215	% the sheaf
9216	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
9217	% $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$.
9218	% By assumption, whenever $V$ is affine we have that $f^{-1}(V)$ is
9219	% affine, see Morphisms, Definition \ref{morphisms-definition-affine}.
9220	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} we conclude that
9221	% $H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0$
9222	% whenever $V$ is affine. Since $S$ has a basis consisting of affine
9223	% opens we win." (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L187--198)
9224	\begin{proof}
9225	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:sheafify_kills_locally_zero, lem:isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero, lem:ext_homcomplex_mathlib, lem:isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_sections_locally_zero, lem:pushforward_sections_functor, lem:rightDerivedNatIso, lem:sectionsFunctorCorepIso, lem:affine_serre_vanishing_general_open, lem:modules_isoSpec_ext_transport, lem:isoSpec_scheme_mathlib, lem:isZero_coyoneda_rightDerived_of_forall_ext_eq_zero, lem:ext_jShriekOU_eq_zero_of_specIso}
9226	  An open immersion of an affine open \(U\) into a separated scheme \(X\) is an
9227	  affine morphism: separatedness makes the immersion \(j\) quasi-separated and the
9228	  preimage of any affine open of \(X\) meets \(U\) in an affine, so \(j\) is affine.
9229	  The relative affine vanishing for affine morphisms then gives \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} =
9230	  0\) for \(q \geq 1\); concretely, by the presheaf description
9231	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}) \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H}\) is the
9232	  sheafification of \(V \mapsto H^q(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{H})\), and for affine \(V\) the
9233	  preimage \(j^{-1}(V)\) is affine, on which the Serre vanishing of
9234	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} kills \(H^q\) for \(q \geq 1\). Since the affine
9235	  opens form a basis of \(X\), the presheaf \(V \mapsto H^q(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{H})\) is
9236	  locally zero, so its sheafification \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H}\) vanishes for \(q \geq 1\).
9237	
9238	  \emph{Proof details.} The argument rests on three identifications.
9239	
9240	  \emph{(1) The cohomology-presheaf identification.} The objectwise homology in degree
9241	  \(n\) of the pushed-forward resolution complex \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) (for a chosen
9242	  injective resolution \(\mathcal{H} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) of the coefficient), evaluated at an open
9243	  \(V\), is identified with the absolute cohomology presheaf
9244	  \(V \mapsto H^n(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{H}) = \operatorname{Ext}^n\bigl(j_!\mathcal{O}_{j^{-1}(V)},
9245	  \mathcal{H}\bigr)\). Concretely: \(\operatorname{Ext}^n(X, Y)\) over an injective resolution of
9246	  the second argument \(Y\) is identified with the \(n\)-th cohomology of the \(\operatorname{Hom}\)
9247	  cochain complex \(H^n\bigl(\operatorname{Hom}(X[0], \mathcal{I}^\bullet)\bigr)\), via the composite
9248	  of the Ext-to-cohomology-class equivalence and the cohomology-class-to-homology equivalence of the
9249	  Hom complex (the equivalences of Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_homcomplex_mathlib}). Taking \(X = j_!\mathcal{O}_{j^{-1}(V)}\) and \(Y =
9250	  \mathcal{H}\), the degree-\(k\) Hom group \(\operatorname{Hom}(j_!\mathcal{O}_{j^{-1}(V)},
9251	  \mathcal{I}^k)\) is identified with \(\Gamma(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{I}^k) = (j_*\mathcal{I}^k)(V)\) by
9252	  the corepresentability \(j_!\mathcal{O}_{j^{-1}(V)} \dashv \Gamma(j^{-1}(V), -)\) of the absolute-cohomology setup,
9253	  degreewise and naturally; the \(\mathbb{N}\)/\(\mathbb{Z}\) indexing of the two cochain complexes is
9254	  matched by the resolution's degreewise comparison isomorphism. The right-derived side is settled by
9255	  the already-used identification of \(R^n j_*\) with the homology of \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\)
9256	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}); no separate ``right-derived equals Ext'' comparison
9257	  lemma is needed, since both compute the same homology over the same injective resolution and meet at
9258	  corepresentability.
9259	
9260	  \emph{(2) Serre vanishing for a general affine open.} The Serre vanishing of
9261	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} is pinned to the \emph{top} open \(\top\) of a spectrum
9262	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R\): it kills \(\operatorname{Ext}^q(j_!\mathcal{O}_\top, -)\) for \(q \geq 1\).
9263	  The cohomology that actually occurs here is over a \emph{general} affine open --- the preimage
9264	  \(j^{-1}(V)\) of an affine open \(V\) of \(X\) that may straddle
9265	  the boundary of \(j(U)\), so \(j^{-1}(V)\) is a general, possibly non-distinguished, affine open of
9266	  \(U\). The residual is therefore \(\operatorname{Ext}^q(j_!\mathcal{O}_{j^{-1}(V)}, \mathcal{H}) = 0\)
9267	  over the abstract affine scheme \(U\), for \(q \geq 1\). We discharge it by splitting it into two
9268	  independent transports.
9269	
9270	  \emph{(2a) The residual lives over an affine scheme.} The immersion \(j\) is an affine morphism:
9271	  for \(U\) affine and \(X\) separated,
9272	  \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\) is affine, so the preimage of every affine open of \(X\) is an affine
9273	  open of \(U\). In particular \(j^{-1}(V) = U \cap V\) is an
9274	  affine open of the affine scheme \(U\). After the reduction to the right-derived sections functor
9275	  --- by the presheaf description (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}) and the
9276	  corepresentability isomorphism of Lemma~\ref{lem:sectionsFunctorCorepIso}, upgraded to right derived
9277	  functors by Lemma~\ref{lem:rightDerivedNatIso} --- the residual reads
9278	  \(\operatorname{Ext}^q_{U}(j_!\mathcal{O}_{j^{-1}(V)}, \mathcal{H}) = 0\), an \(\operatorname{Ext}\)
9279	  vanishing in the module category of the abstract affine scheme \(U\), for \(q \geq 1\).
9280	
9281	  \emph{(2b) Transport along the whole-scheme spectrum isomorphism (Need~\#1).} The abstract affine
9282	  scheme \(U\) carries the canonical \emph{whole-scheme} isomorphism
9283	  \(U \cong \operatorname{Spec}\Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_U)\) (\(\operatorname{Scheme.isoSpec}\),
9284	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isoSpec_scheme_mathlib}), a genuine isomorphism of schemes because \(U\) is affine.
9285	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isoSpec_ext_transport} it induces an equivalence of module categories
9286	  \(U.\mathrm{Modules} \simeq (\operatorname{Spec}\Gamma U).\mathrm{Modules}\) and transports
9287	  \(\operatorname{Ext}\) along it (via \(\operatorname{Ext.mapExactFunctor}\)), carrying the residual to
9288	  \(\operatorname{Ext}^q_{\operatorname{Spec}\Gamma U}(j_!\mathcal{O}_{V'}, \mathcal{H}') = 0\), where
9289	  \(V'\) is the image affine open of \(j^{-1}(V)\) and \(\mathcal{H}'\) the transported (still
9290	  quasi-coherent) coefficient. Under the equivalence \(V'\) is in general a non-distinguished affine
9291	  open of \(\operatorname{Spec}\Gamma U\).
9292	
9293	  \emph{(2c) General-affine-open Serre vanishing (Need~\#2).} Over \(\operatorname{Spec}\Gamma U\) the
9294	  open \(V'\) is a general affine open, and Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing_general_open} gives
9295	  \(\operatorname{Ext}^q(j_!\mathcal{O}_{V'}, \mathcal{H}') = 0\) for \(q \geq 1\) and every affine
9296	  open \(V'\) and quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{H}'\). This is read off from the basis-comparison criterion
9297	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis}) with the cover system's basis \(\mathcal{B}\) enlarged
9298	  from the distinguished opens to \emph{all} affine opens; it requires no fresh cover system on the
9299	  open \(V'\) and stays entirely inside \((\operatorname{Spec}\Gamma U).\mathrm{Modules}\). Combining
9300	  (2b) and (2c) kills the residual.
9301	
9302	  \textit{Remark (a blocked approach).} One might try to make \(j^{-1}(V)\) the \emph{whole} space by
9303	  transporting along the spectrum isomorphism of the \emph{open subscheme}
9304	  \(j^{-1}(V) \cong \operatorname{Spec}\Gamma(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{O})\). This is a dead end: the
9305	  inclusion \(j^{-1}(V) \hookrightarrow U\) is an open immersion, not an isomorphism, so there is no
9306	  equivalence \(U.\mathrm{Modules} \simeq (j^{-1}(V)).\mathrm{Modules}\) to transport
9307	  \(\operatorname{Ext}\) along; using the open-subscheme isomorphism forces a restriction functor
9308	  \(\mathcal{H} \mapsto \mathcal{H}|_{j^{-1}(V)}\), whose derived comparison
9309	  \(\operatorname{Ext}^q_{U}(j_!\mathcal{O}_{j^{-1}(V)}, \mathcal{H}) \cong H^q(j^{-1}(V),
9310	  \mathcal{H}|_{j^{-1}(V)})\) is exactly ``restriction preserves injectives'' --- infrastructure not
9311	  available in the present formalism. The sound route therefore keeps the spectrum isomorphism at the
9312	  level of the \emph{whole} affine \(U\) as in~(2b) and handles the general affine open ambiently
9313	  via~(2c).
9314	
9315	  \emph{(3) Locally zero implies zero sheafification.} The
9316	  passage from ``\(H^q(\cdots) = 0\) on a basis of affine opens'' to ``\(R^q j_* = 0\)'' is the
9317	  module-sheafification analogue of Lemma~\ref{lem:sheafify_kills_locally_zero} (and of its packaged
9318	  form Lemma~\ref{lem:isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero}). It is obtained by transporting the
9319	  abelian-sheaf statement across the natural isomorphism
9320	  \(\operatorname{toSheaf} \circ \operatorname{sheafify} \cong \operatorname{presheafToSheaf} \circ
9321	  \operatorname{forget}\) between module-sheafification followed by the forgetful functor and abelian
9322	  sheafification; the ``locally zero'' hypothesis it consumes is exactly the basis-of-affines vanishing
9323	  supplied by identifications (1) and~(2) above.
9324	\end{proof}
9325	
9326	\begin{lemma}
9327	[Pushing forward an affine open immersion degenerates the composite direct image]
9328	  \label{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
9329	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp}
9330	  \uses{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_acyclic, lem:injective_of_adjoint,
9331	    def:right_acyclic, lem:right_derived_vanishes_injective,
9332	    lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived, lem:right_derived_injective_resolution,
9333	    lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self, lem:scheme_pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_mathlib,
9334	    lem:restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib, lem:scheme_pushforwardComp_mathlib}
9335	  Let \(X\) be separated, \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\) the open immersion of an affine
9336	  open \(U\), and \(f : X \to S\) any morphism, with composite \(g := f \circ j : U \to S\).
9337	  Then for every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module \(\mathcal{H}\) and every \(k \geq 0\),
9338	  \[
9339	    R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr) \;\cong\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H}.
9340	  \]
9341	\end{lemma}
9342	\begin{proof}
9343	  \uses{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_acyclic, lem:injective_of_adjoint,
9344	    def:right_acyclic, lem:right_derived_vanishes_injective,
9345	    lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived, lem:right_derived_injective_resolution,
9346	    lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self, lem:scheme_pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_mathlib,
9347	    lem:restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib, lem:scheme_pushforwardComp_mathlib}
9348	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{H} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
9349	  \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-modules and apply the pushforward \(j_*\) degreewise, forming the complex
9350	  \(K := j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. We verify the hypotheses of the
9351	  acyclic-resolution comparison (Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived}) for the
9352	  functor \(G = f_*\) and the candidate \(f_*\)-acyclic resolution \(K\) of \(j_*\mathcal{H}\), then
9353	  transport along the pushforward-composition isomorphism. The argument is the
9354	  formalization-friendly categorical route; it establishes the \(f_*\)-acyclicity of each
9355	  \(j_*\mathcal{I}^n\) by an adjoint-preserves-injectives argument and is an equivalent, cleaner
9356	  alternative to the presheaf-description argument of the Stacks Project (which would compute
9357	  cohomology over the opens \(U \cap f^{-1}(V)\); these are general opens of the affine \(U\) and
9358	  need not be affine, since \(f^{-1}(V)\) is open but not affine in \(X\), so that route re-enters
9359	  the restriction-of-injectives obstruction we deliberately avoid).
9360	
9361	  \emph{(b) Each \(j_*\mathcal{I}^n\) is \(f_*\)-acyclic.} The pushforward
9362	  \(j_* = \operatorname{pushforward} j\) preserves injective objects. Indeed, it is the right
9363	  adjoint in the adjunction \(\operatorname{pullback} j \dashv \operatorname{pushforward} j\)
9364	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:scheme_pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_mathlib}), and its left adjoint
9365	  \(\operatorname{pullback} j\) preserves monomorphisms: for an open immersion \(j\) the pullback
9366	  functor is isomorphic to the restriction functor,
9367	  \(\operatorname{restrictFunctor} j \cong \operatorname{pullback} j\)
9368	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}), and restriction of a sheaf of modules to an
9369	  open subscheme is exact, hence preserves monos. By the criterion that the right adjoint of a
9370	  mono-preserving left adjoint preserves injectives (Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_of_adjoint}), each
9371	  \(j_*\mathcal{I}^n\) is injective in \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\). An injective object is
9372	  right-\(G\)-acyclic for \emph{any} additive functor \(G\)
9373	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:right_derived_vanishes_injective}, in the sense of
9374	  Definition~\ref{def:right_acyclic}), so \(j_*\mathcal{I}^n\) is \(f_*\)-acyclic:
9375	  \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n) = 0\) for all \(k \geq 1\). No Serre vanishing on
9376	  \(U \cap f^{-1}(V)\) is needed, and the restriction-of-injectives wall is avoided.
9377	
9378	  \emph{(a) \(K\) is exact in positive degrees.} The objectwise homology of the pushed-forward
9379	  resolution \(K = j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in degree \(k\) computes the right-derived functor of
9380	  \(j_*\) along the injective resolution \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet\): by
9381	  Lemma~\ref{lem:right_derived_injective_resolution},
9382	  \[
9383	    H^k\bigl(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\bigr) \;\cong\; \bigl(R^k j_*\bigr)(\mathcal{H})
9384	      \;=\; R^k j_*\mathcal{H}.
9385	  \]
9386	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_acyclic} (\(j\) affine, \(\mathcal{H}\) quasi-coherent
9387	  on the affine \(U\)) this vanishes for \(k \geq 1\); hence \(K\) is exact at every positive degree
9388	  \(n+1\).
9389	
9390	  \emph{Augmentation: \(j_*\mathcal{H} \cong K.\mathrm{cycles}\,0\).} The pushforward \(j_*\) is left
9391	  exact, being a right adjoint (Lemma~\ref{lem:scheme_pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_mathlib}) and so
9392	  finite-limit-preserving. For a left-exact functor the zeroth right-derived functor is the functor
9393	  itself, \(R^0 j_* \cong j_*\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self}), and the degree-zero
9394	  cycles of the applied resolution compute \(R^0 j_*\); thus \(j_*\) carries the resolution
9395	  augmentation \(\mathcal{H} \cong (\mathcal{I}^\bullet).\mathrm{cycles}\,0\) to
9396	  \[
9397	    j_*\mathcal{H} \;\cong\; H^0\bigl(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\bigr)
9398	      \;=\; K.\mathrm{cycles}\,0,
9399	  \]
9400	  the augmentation that makes \(K\) a resolution of \(j_*\mathcal{H}\).
9401

9404	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived}) applied with \(G = f_*\) gives, for every
9405	  \(k\), an isomorphism

9413	  f \cong \operatorname{pushforward}(j \circ f)\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:scheme_pushforwardComp_mathlib})
9414	  identifies \(f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)\) with \((f \circ j)_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet = g_*\mathcal{I}^
9415	  \bullet\); applying it degreewise and taking homology, while recognising \(H^k(g_*\mathcal{I}^
9416	  \bullet) \cong R^k g_*\mathcal{H}\) via the same injective-resolution identification
9417	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:right_derived_injective_resolution}), yields

9422	  \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{H}) \cong R^k g_*\mathcal{H}\).

8	\section{Setup and motivation}
9	
10	This chapter records the derived-functor higher direct images \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\)
11	for \(i \geq 1\). It provides the single object that the {\v C}ech computation of
12	Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage} compares against: the comparison
13	theorem \(\ref{lem:cech_computes_cohomology}\) identifies the cohomology of the
14	relative {\v C}ech complex with \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) wherever the derived functor
15	is defined.
16	
17	We use the classical cohomology-sheaf formulation (not the derived category).
18	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a
19	quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module.
20	
21	\section{Definition of the higher direct images}
22	
23	\begin{definition}\leanok
24	[Higher direct image]
25	  \label{def:higher_direct_image}
26	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage}
27	  % NOTE (iter-233): the Lean `higherDirectImage` carries an explicit instance
28	  % hypothesis `[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]`, because Mathlib has
29	  % `Abelian X.Modules` but NOT `EnoughInjectives` / `IsGrothendieckAbelian` for
30	  % `SheafOfModules` over a sheaf of rings (the Grothendieck-abelian instance is
31	  % only for `Sheaf J A` with a fixed value category). The definition is therefore
32	  % conditional; the hypothesis can be dropped once a lane proves that instance.
33	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
34	  % `lemma-relative-affine-vanishing' (proof), describing $R^i f_*$ as the
35	  % $i$-th right derived functor of $f_*$ via the sheafified presheaf
36	  % $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$
37	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L188--192).
38	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "According to
39	  % Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
40	  % the sheaf
41	  % $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
42	  % $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$."
43	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, \S\,Quasi-coherence of
44	  higher direct images (and the underlying Cohomology chapter).}
45	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a
46	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. For \(i \geq 0\) the \emph{\(i\)-th
47	  higher direct image} \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) is the \(i\)-th right derived functor
48	  of the pushforward functor \(f_*\) applied to \(\mathcal{F}\). Concretely,
49	  \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) is the \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module obtained as the
50	  sheafification of the presheaf
51	  \[
52	    V \;\longmapsto\; H^i\bigl(f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
53	    \qquad V \subset S \text{ open},
54	  \]
55	  where \(H^i(-, -)\) denotes sheaf cohomology. For \(i = 0\) this recovers the
56	  ordinary pushforward \(R^0 f_*\mathcal{F} = f_*\mathcal{F}\).
57	\end{definition}

#### Rewritten `lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp` part-(2) — CORRECT

The previous proof (Serre vanishing on the affine `U ∩ f⁻¹V`) was flawed: `U ∩ f⁻¹V` is a
pullback of open immersions and is generally NOT affine even when U and V are affine. The iter-066
rewrite correctly abandons that route entirely.

The new categorical route is mathematically sound:

1. **`pullback j ≅ restrictFunctor j`** (anchored by `lem:restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib`,
   rdep=4) — The restriction functor along an open immersion preserves monomorphisms (it is
   exact), so `pullback j` is exact and mono-preserving.

2. **`pushforward j` preserves injectives** — By `CategoryTheory.Injective.injective_of_adjoint`
   (anchored by `lem:injective_of_adjoint`, rdep=3, mathlibok): if `L ⊣ R` and `L` is exact
   (equivalently: preserves monomorphisms), then `R` sends injectives to injectives. Since
   `pullback j ⊣ pushforward j` and `pullback j` is exact, `j_* I` is injective whenever `I` is.

3. **`j_* I^n` is `f_*`-acyclic** — Injective objects are acyclic for any right-derived functor
   (anchored by `lem:right_derived_vanishes_injective`, present in AcyclicResolution chapter).
   This closes **`hacyc`**.

4. **`hexact` (`H^k(j_* I^•) = 0` for `k ≥ 1`)** — Direct application of
   `lem:open_immersion_pushforward_acyclic` (now axiom-clean, has_sorry=False) to each `I^n`.
   Well-specified.

5. **`eRes` (`j_* H ≅ K.cycles 0`)** — `R⁰ j_* ≅ j_*` for a left-exact right adjoint is
   standard (anchored by `lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self`, AcyclicResolution chapter). Combined
   with the exactness of the augmented complex at degree 0, this identifies `j_* H` with the
   zeroth cycles of `j_* I^•`. Well-specified.

6. **`transport`** — `pushforwardComp j f` (anchored by `lem:scheme_pushforwardComp_mathlib`)
   identifies `f_*(j_* I^•) ≅ g_* I^•` objectwise; then `isoRightDerivedObj` (anchored by
   `lem:right_derived_injective_resolution`, AcyclicResolution chapter) compares the derived
   functors. Well-specified.

**All four obligations at formalizable detail.** The `\uses{}` list for
`lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp` covers all supporting anchors (10 entries: acyclic,
injective_of_adjoint, def:right_acyclic, right_derived_vanishes_injective,

restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib, scheme_pushforwardComp_mathlib). Dependency graph is clean.
